description: Classical scale invariance at tree level: no explicit mass scales in the Lagrangian.
Radiative symmetry breaking via Coleman-Weinberg mechanism at one loop; higher-order corrections neglected.
No fermions in the dark sector. Deliberate minimal choice for FOPT+GW production.
No SM coupling. Dark SU(2) sector has no portal coupling to the Standard Model.
Perturbative regime holds (g < 4*pi). Satisfied for all preferred points (g < 1.1).
No U(1)_Y factor. Pure SU(2)_X without hypercharge.
No RG running of couplings. Fixed renormalization scale set to chi0.
High-temperature (small-field) expansion of one-loop finite-T effective potential. Valid when m(phi)/T << 1.
Gaussian approximation for false-vacuum fraction used for percolation.
No Daisy resummation included in thermal effective potential.
Renormalization scale dependence. Scale set to chi0; sensitivity to this choice not quantified.
Scheme dependence of the one-loop CW calculation. Different renormalization schemes give different finite parts.
Uncertainty in nucleation temperature calculation. Backend uses simplified bounce action method without cross-validation.
198/680 FOPT scan points (29%) are numerical failures. Viable region may be biased.
dbf (dissipative bulk flow) template (arXiv:2511.15687) selected for GW spectrum. Designed for strong transitions with alpha >> 1.
Alternative template (higgsless) considered but discarded. No sound-wave-only template used for moderate alpha.
Wall velocity v_w derived from FOPT pipeline, not a free parameter. Affects kinetic energy fraction and GW amplitude.
Sound-wave vs bulk-flow lifetime not explicitly modeled. For large alpha, bubble collisions may dominate in PTA band.
Efficiency factors (kappa) for kinetic energy conversion not explicitly documented. Internal to dbf template.
PTArcade priors: Uniform(g) in [0.5, 3.0], Uniform(log10(chi0)) in [-5, 0.699]. Post-hoc truncation based on FOPT scan.
Log-uniform prior on chi0 over 5 orders of magnitude. Broad but natural for scale-invariant model.
Single MCMC chain with 7501 effective samples. Standard requires multiple chains with Gelman-Rubin R-1 < 0.05.
PTArcade ceffyl mode refits to free-spectrum KDEs. Faster but assumes KDEs are accurate approximations.
NANOGrav 15yr ceffyl analysis does not include Hellings-Downs correlations (corr=False).
Eternal inflation filter uses 3 + Tp*I_prime(Tp) < 0. Standard criterion is Gamma/H^4 < 9/(4*pi).
Eternal inflation check uses Tp (percolation temperature). Tn or T* might be more appropriate.
Factor ~33 alpha discrepancy with arXiv:2109.11558 BP1 (ours: 15, theirs: 0.45). Beta/H matches within 1%.
Delta N_eff constraint flagged but NOT resolved. Could invalidate model if dark sector was in equilibrium with SM.
DM overproduction from stable gauge bosons flagged but NOT resolved. Minimal model is cosmologically inviable without portal.
SIDM and CMB annihilation constraints flagged but NOT resolved. Subleading to overproduction issue.
BBN bound partially checked. Best benchmarks safe but marginal points with T_p < 1 MeV exist.
Extreme alpha values (10^3 to 10^33) across viable region may indicate HT expansion breakdown at high end.
FOPT scan uses sparse grid (680 points, 100x log chi0, 20x linear g). May miss fine features.
f_peak_est is null for many FOPT benchmark points, limiting frequency prediction capability.
Model not novel: arXiv:2109.11558 studied exact same model for same target signal.
Unverifiable paper IDs in earlier handoff stages flagged and corrected by librarian.
dbf template reference (arXiv:2511.15687) cannot be verified through public searches.
Gaussian approximation reference (arXiv:2602.02928) cannot be verified. Standard technique but reference invalid.
Tension between best scan points (chi0 ~ 0.4-0.5 GeV) and MAP posterior (chi0 = 1.12 GeV) not discussed.
SIDM and supernova cooling constraints flagged as recast_needed but not performed.
Hubble/g_star interpolated from table with extrapolation at extreme temperatures. Standard approach.
Numerical derivatives use h = 1e-3*x step size. Convergence of derivative accuracy not validated.
